pi_items.source: Task description specifies this name to be created. Floreant POS documentation confirms support for modifier groups with custom names. Realistic restaurant menu terminology.
Task description specifies this name to be created. 'Build-Your-Own' is standard restaurant terminology for customizable items. Floreant POS supports custom menu item names.
Task specification. $9.99 is realistic restaurant pricing for a burger item. Uses common X.99 psychological pricing.
Task specification. Bacon is a common burger topping at $1.50 - realistic modifier price.
Task specification. Avocado is a premium burger topping at $2.00 - realistic modifier price.
Task specification. Extra Cheese at $0.75 is realistic for a common burger topping modifier.
Mathematical verification: $9.99 (burger) + $1.50 (Bacon) + $2.00 (Avocado) = $13.49. Note: Extra Cheese ($0.75) is not selected per task instructions. Calculation is mathematically correct.
A ±$0.10 tolerance is appropriate for exact price matching in 2-decimal currency systems. Accounts for minor rounding variations.

privileged_info_summary: The verify_menu_order_workflow task uses Floreant POS (open-source Java restaurant point-of-sale system) with synthetic data created during task execution. Task creates a modifier group named 'Burger Toppings' with three modifiers: Bacon ($1.50), Avocado ($2.00), and Extra Cheese ($0.75). A menu item called 'Build-Your-Own Burger' is created at $9.99. The task then places an order selecting Bacon and Avocado modifiers, resulting in an expected subtotal of $13.49 (verified calculation: $9.99 + $1.50 + $2.00). All prices are realistic restaurant values, and all claims are mathematically correct.
